# daytona_admin_organization_region_quota (Resource)

Manages Daytona organization region quotas through Daytona admin APIs. This resource requires credentials with Daytona admin privileges.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "daytona_admin_organization_region_quota" "example" {
  organization_id    = "organization-id"
  region_id          = "region-id"
  sandbox_class      = "container"
  total_cpu_quota    = 32
  total_memory_quota = 64
  total_disk_quota   = 1024
  total_gpu_quota    = 0
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `organization_id` (String) Daytona organization ID.
- `region_id` (String) Daytona region ID where the quota applies.
- `sandbox_class` (String) Sandbox class for this quota. Supported values are: linux-vm, container, android, windows.
- `total_cpu_quota` (Number) Total CPU quota.
- `total_disk_quota` (Number) Total disk quota.
- `total_gpu_quota` (Number) Total GPU quota.
- `total_memory_quota` (Number) Total memory quota.

### Optional

- `allowed_gpu_types` (List of String) Allowed GPU types for this quota. Supported values are: H100, RTX-PRO-6000.
- `max_cpu_per_gpu_sandbox` (Number) Maximum CPU per GPU sandbox.
- `max_cpu_per_sandbox` (Number) Maximum CPU per sandbox.
- `max_disk_per_gpu_sandbox` (Number) Maximum disk per GPU sandbox.
- `max_disk_per_non_ephemeral_sandbox` (Number) Maximum disk per non-ephemeral sandbox.
- `max_disk_per_sandbox` (Number) Maximum disk per sandbox.
- `max_memory_per_gpu_sandbox` (Number) Maximum memory per GPU sandbox.
- `max_memory_per_sandbox` (Number) Maximum memory per sandbox.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Quota identifier in `organization_id:region_id:sandbox_class` format.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import daytona_admin_organization_region_quota.example organization-id:region-id:container
```
